mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates robust residential demand and construction activity, which boosts economic growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration shows no statistically significant predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 195.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing uncertainty and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and stable or declining unemployment rates signals a tightening labor market with strong job creation, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 105.0 threshold indicates a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, which signals economic deceleration and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO drops below 105.0 during late-cycle phases.
